name: normalization
description: Cross-SDK matching-time normalization (┬¦6). Each case pins the exact output of normalizeForMatching / normalize_for_matching (NFKC + zero-width strip + curated confusable fold) for a given input. Both SDK harnesses (sdk/tests/unit/normalization.test.ts, sdk-python/tests/test_normalization.py) feed every `input` through their normalizer and must produce the pinned `normalized` string byte-for-byte. `matches_override` additionally pins that a keyword rule for "override" fires on the normalized copy while the raw input would have dodged it. A divergence is a release blocker unless recorded in conformance/known-divergences.md. This is a MATCHING-only transform: it never mutates stored/forwarded content.

cases.note: Plain ASCII is unchanged ŌĆö normalization never perturbs existing behavior.
U+200D ZERO WIDTH JOINER spliced into the keyword is stripped.
ZWSP, soft hyphen, BOM, word joiner all stripped.
Fullwidth Latin letters fold to ASCII under NFKC.
Cyrillic small o (U+043E) folds to Latin o; NFKC alone would not.
Greek small omicron (U+03BF) folds to Latin o.
Confusable + zero-width combined still collapses to the keyword.
Bidirectional format controls (LRM/RLM, embeddings/overrides, isolates) are invisible and must be stripped before matching.
Non-confusable characters (incl. astral/emoji) pass through unchanged.
No keyword present after normalization ŌĆö must not falsely match.
